prompts:
from the comp writer fish

Prompt 1 — Install deps and test CompFish:

Pull CompFish from VPS and test it. Run these commands:

scp root@fishbrain.meatbag.com.au:/root/perthfish/agents/compfish/*.py ~/compfish/
cd ~/compfish
pip install requests beautifulsoup4 lxml
python3 compfish_db.py
python3 compfish_daemon.py scrape
python3 compfish_daemon.py report

Tell me how many comps it found and if there were any errors.

Prompt 2 — Set up Playwright for JS-rendered sites:

Install Playwright in ~/compfish for headless browser scraping:

pip install playwright
playwright install chromium

Then test it works:
python3 -c "from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright; p = sync_playwright().start(); b = p.chromium.launch(); page = b.new_page(); page.goto('https://www.competitionsguide.com.au/win-cars/'); print(f'Title: {page.title()}'); print(f'Links: {len(page.query_selector_all(\"a\"))}'); b.close(); p.stop()"

Tell me the title and link count.

Prompt 3 — Set up cron for auto-scraping:

Set up a cron job to run CompFish every 6 hours:

crontab -e

Add this line:
0 */6 * * * cd ~/compfish && python3 compfish_daemon.py grind >> compfish_cron.log 2>&1

Verify with: crontab -l

from comp writer fish

SkyBox is the one with Playwright — run it there. PerthFish cron is redundant now since SkyBox can hit all 4 scrapers. Tell CodeFish:

Prompt 4 — Add SkyBox cron and kill PerthFish one:

Add the CompFish cron on SkyBox:

crontab -e
Add: 0 */6 * * * cd ~/compfish && ./venv/bin/python3 compfish_daemon.py grind >> compfish_cron.log 2>&1

Verify with: crontab -l

Then SSH to PerthFish and remove the old cron:
ssh root@fishbrain.meatbag.com.au "crontab -l" 
Find and remove the compfish line. Show me the before and after.

Prompt 5 — Wire Playwright into the 3 broken scrapers:

The CompFish scraper at ~/compfish/compfish_scraper.py has 4 scrapers but only aussiecomps works. The other 3 (competitionsguide, netrewards, competitioncloud) need Playwright because they're JS-rendered.

Update compfish_scraper.py to:

1. Add a playwright_scrape() helper function that:
   - Launches headless Chromium
   - Loads a URL, waits for content
   - Returns the fully rendered HTML as a BeautifulSoup object
   - Closes the browser

2. Update scrape_competitionsguide() to use playwright_scrape() instead of requests.get()
   - The page at https://www.competitionsguide.com.au/win-cars/ has competition cards loaded via JS
   - Look for links containing competition titles and prize values
   - Scrape these category pages: win-cars, win-cash, win-overseas-holidays, win-electronics, best-odds, 60-second-entry

3. Update scrape_netrewards() to use playwright_scrape()
   - URL: https://netrewards.com.au/competitions-category/online-free-entry/

4. Update scrape_competitioncloud() to use playwright_scrape()
   - URL: https://www.competitioncloud.com.au

5. Keep scrape_aussiecomps() as-is (works with requests)

After updating, run: cd ~/compfish && ./venv/bin/python3 compfish_daemon.py scrape

Tell me the total comps found across all 4 scrapers.

That'll unlock all 4 sources. Once CodeFish reports back with the numbers, I'll build the form-filling engine prompts.
